name: noop
description: The standalone lodash.noop package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want a named no-op helper without adding a dependency.
tagline: A function that intentionally does nothing.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.noop file in both raw and normalized forms. The value is to preserve a familiar tiny helper locally when existing code already expects it.
nativeAlternative: An empty arrow function is trivial to write, but vendoring the known helper can still be useful when replacing an existing dependency.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
